title: Using a U_VERSION Field in a Server Page
breadcrumbs: Using a U_VERSION Field in a Server Page
text: Using a U_VERSION Field in a Server Page Data integrity can be maintained using the U_VERSION field in a web application. You can copy the U_VERSION field to a hidden non-database field that is sent to the browser. When handling a request, compare the hidden field with the U_VERSION to decide if the modified data can be written to the database. The following is an example of using U_VERSION to maintain the integrity of entity TIMESHEET. In the entity TIMESHEET, define a database field U_VERSION whose data type is String with packing code C1. In the entity TIMESHEET, define non-database field DUMMY whose data type is String with packing code C1 and with syntax HID. Define a server page TEST_UVERSION. Load fields of TIMESHEET in TEST_UVERSION except the U_VERSION field. In TEST_UVERSION, add a button (SAVE) for submitting a request from a browser to the server for saving the modified occurrences. Define an operation: operation carry_on webget webgen end Copy Set the Activation URL property of TIMESHEET as: TIMESHEET.carry_on. Waiting for developers. Activation URL property may not be in v9. Define an function: function save_u_version clear/e "TIMESHEET" retrieve/e "TIMESHEET" $1=1 while($1-1 < $hits("TIMESHEET")) setocc "TIMESHET", $1 DUMMY=U_VERSION $1 = $1+1 endwhile end Copy In the exec operation of TIMESHEET, add: call save_u_version webgen Copy In the Details trigger of button SAVE, add: store/e "TIMESHEET" call save_u_version Copy In the write trigger of entity TIMESHEET, add: if (DUMMY = U_VERSION) write endif Copy Related Links U_VERSION for Improving Locking Performance Uniface supports a version mechanism which can improve performance by using a field called U_VERSION. U_VERSION is a one-byte field which you must assign to an entity for it to work. In other words, it is not automatically available. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
